stem: Tom (Series-B SaaS founder): 'We already use Brex — why would we switch?' Which family?
feedback: competitor_brex is the §12.2 Tom archetype's top objection (GC-06, L.D. verbatim, §10.2).

lo: LO-M2.2 · restate in buyer's own words
stem: Emma (manufacturing ops head): 'Our accountant won't deal with another tool.' Which response best restates in her own words without rebutting?

options.text: Our Xero sync is automated — your accountant won't add work.
So you're protecting your accountant's bandwidth.
Most accountants love us once they see the integration.
Have you tried showing your accountant our integration page?

feedback: B uses Emma's noun (accountant) and reframes her concern (bandwidth-protection) with no rebuttal — pattern from M.G. §10.1. A argues; C dismisses; D deflects (GC-12).

stem: Lukas just told you he doesn't trust fintechs after a payroll provider went sideways. You restated cleanly. What's the follow-up question?

options.text: Have you considered our regulatory backing with a tier-1 partner bank?
What specifically went sideways with that payroll provider?
Are you open to a quick demo if I send our DPA?
Most operators feel that way before they try us — fair to share what changed for them?

feedback: B surfaces the actual broken thing (LO-M2.3, M.G. §10.1). A is M6 regulatory deflection (wrong module). C closes prematurely. D is third-party-proof, not buyer-specific.
